import {
  ColorScales,
  TypographySamples,
  RadiusSamples,
} from "./theme-demo";

# Theme & Design Tokens

Patch UI is Tailwind CSS v4 native. The token system is a single stylesheet (`tokens.css`) that does three jobs at once: defines raw CSS custom properties, bridges them into Tailwind through an `@theme inline` block, and provides text-style utility classes for compound typography. Light and dark switch from a single `.dark` class.

## How the layers fit together

```
tokens.css
├── Layer 1: raw CSS custom properties on :root
│   └── .dark { …overrides }
├── Layer 2: @theme inline block
│   └── makes each token available as bg-*, text-*, rounded-*, shadow-*
└── Layer 3: @layer utilities
    └── .text-heading-40, .text-copy-14, .text-button-14, etc.
```

You can write against any layer:

```tsx
// Layer 1: direct CSS variable
<div style={{ background: "var(--gray-1000)" }} />

// Layer 2: Tailwind utility
<div className="bg-gray-1000" />

// Layer 3: compound typography utility (size + line-height + tracking + weight)
<p className="text-copy-14">Body text.</p>
```

Components read from layers 2 and 3 exclusively. Layer 1 is for you, if you need it.

## Color scales

Every scale runs `100` (lightest fill) to `1000` (heaviest text) and inverts in dark mode. The intent is consistent across scales:

| Step | Role |
|------|------|
| `100` | subtle fill (hover-adjacent, callout background) |
| `200` | hover fill |
| `300` | active fill |
| `400` | border |
| `500` | hover border |
| `600` | active border |
| `700` | solid (button background, saturated fill) |
| `800` | solid hover |
| `900` | secondary text |
| `1000` | primary text |

<ColorScales />

### Gray alpha

Gray alpha tokens are the same scale but expressed as translucent black (light mode) or white (dark mode). Use them when a fill needs to sit on any background color, such as a hover state that must work over both surface tones. Range: `--gray-alpha-100` through `--gray-alpha-1000`.

### Backgrounds

Two-tone canvas scale for surface elevation:

| Token | Light | Dark |
|-------|-------|------|
| `--background-100` | `#ffffff` | `#000000` |
| `--background-200` | `#fafafa` | `#0a0a0a` |

In dark mode both are near-black; the Vercel Geist approach is to use border + shadow (not brightness) for surface differentiation.

### Semantic roles

Fixed hex values that read identically in both themes, because status intent shouldn't shift meaning:

| Token | Value | Purpose |
|-------|-------|---------|
| `--error` | `#ea001d` | destructive Button, Toast, Field.error |
| `--warning` | `#f5a623` | caution Button, Toast |
| `--success` | `#15963f` | positive Toast, confirmed status |

Each has `-hover` and `-active` companions.

## Typography

Four families, all size-numbered so the class name doubles as documentation:

- `text-heading-*` — semibold display type (weight 600), for section headers
- `text-copy-*` — body text (weight 400), for paragraphs and prose
- `text-label-*` — dense/UI text (weight 400), tighter line-heights, for form labels and metadata
- `text-button-*` — control text (weight 500), single-line, for interactive labels

Each class applies size, line-height, tracking, and weight in one shot.

<TypographySamples />

## Radius

Four values only. Controls use `radius-6`, surfaces use `radius-12`, marketing chrome uses `radius-16`, chips use `radius-full`.

<RadiusSamples />

## Shadows

Elevation is otherwise flat with 1px hairline borders. Shadows appear only on overlays.

| Token | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| `--shadow-card` | marketing cards, promoted surfaces |
| `--shadow-menu` | popovers, menus, selects, tooltips |
| `--shadow-modal` | dialogs, sheets |
| `--shadow-tooltip` | alias for `--shadow-menu` |

Dark-mode shadows are deeper (heavier alpha) so overlays still separate from a near-black canvas.

## Motion

One easing and two durations cover the whole library:

| Token | Value | Purpose |
|-------|-------|---------|
| `--ease-standard` | `cubic-bezier(0.175, 0.885, 0.32, 1.1)` | slight overshoot; feels alive |
| `--duration-state` | `75ms` | hover / active color transitions |
| `--duration-overlay` | `150ms` | popover, menu, modal enter/exit |

Under `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`, both durations collapse to `0ms` and easing becomes `ease` automatically. Components inherit this without special-casing.

## Focus

| Token | Value |
|-------|-------|
| `--focus-ring-color` | `var(--gray-alpha-600)` |
| `--focus-ring-offset` | `1px` |

Consumed through the shared `focusRing` recipe. A 1px neutral outline reads as intentional across both themes. No `ring-2` or `ring-3` anywhere.

## Spacing

The v4 approach: the token file exposes `--space-4`, `--space-8`, `--space-12`, `--space-16`, `--space-24`, `--space-32`, `--space-40`, `--space-64`, `--space-96` as raw variables. Tailwind's default `p-*`, `m-*`, `gap-*`, `h-*` utilities are unchanged and use the standard `0.25rem` multiplier (so `p-4` = `1rem`). Reach for the raw `var(--space-N)` variables only when you need exact pixel alignment inside a token-critical layout (e.g. matching a design that specifies `24px` explicitly).

## Light and dark

Tokens are defined on `:root` (light) and overridden inside `.dark` (dark). Toggle by adding the class to `<html>`:

```html
<html class="dark">
```

No JavaScript required. The `theme-toggle` component (`npx shadcn add @patchui/theme-toggle`) handles system-preference detection and persistence if you want a UI switch.

## Customizing

Every token is a plain CSS variable. Override in your own CSS after importing the tokens:

```css
/* app/globals.css */
@import "tailwindcss";
@import "./styles/patch-ui-tokens.css";

:root {
  /* Rebrand the primary text color across every component */
  --gray-1000: #0a0a0a;
}

.dark {
  --gray-1000: #f5f5f5;
}
```

Because components read from these variables at runtime, your overrides take effect everywhere. No rebuild required.

If you edit a color that affects readable text (`gray-800`, `gray-900`, `gray-1000` against the backgrounds), run `npm run check:contrast -w packages/react` to verify you haven't broken the [contrast contract](/docs/contrast).
